description: Fix critical watch-mode cache bug in plugin-tools and edge cases in plugin-sdk fetch utilities

userStories.title: Fix watch-mode module cache causing stale plugin code on alternating rebuilds
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using watch mode, I want every rebuild to load the latest plugin code so that tools.json and the IIFE bundle always reflect the current source.
acceptanceCriteria: Each watch-mode rebuild uses a unique, monotonically increasing cache key that never repeats
The 3rd, 4th, and subsequent rebuilds all load fresh plugin code, not stale cached modules
Module cache entries do not grow unboundedly (old cache entries are naturally garbage collected or bounded)
userStories.notes: File: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts, line 1034-1036. The current code: `pluginCacheKey = (pluginCacheKey + 1) % 2;` alternates between 0 and 1. On the 3rd build, cache key cycles back to 1, and Node.js returns the cached module from the 1st build (same URL `?t=1`). Fix: use a monotonically increasing counter instead of modulo 2. Change to `pluginCacheKey++` (or use Date.now()). Node.js ESM module cache entries for `file:///...?t=N` URLs will accumulate but each is small (just the module namespace object). For a typical watch session (dozens of rebuilds), this is negligible. The variable `pluginCacheKey` is declared at module scope — find it (search for `let pluginCacheKey`) and change the increment from `(pluginCacheKey + 1) % 2` to just `pluginCacheKey + 1` or `++pluginCacheKey`.

userStories.title: Fix tsc spawn failing on Windows due to missing shell execution
userStories.description: As a Windows plugin developer, I want opentabs-plugin build to find and run tsc correctly so that the build command does not fail with ENOENT.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnSync for tsc uses shell: true or platformExec('tsc') to correctly resolve .cmd files on Windows
The PATH augmentation (envWithBin) is preserved so project-local tsc is found
Build command works on both Unix and Windows (no regression on Unix)
userStories.notes: File: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts, line 1010. `spawnSync('tsc', [], { cwd: projectDir, env: envWithBin })` — on Windows, `spawnSync` without `shell: true` cannot find `tsc.cmd` in `node_modules/.bin/`. The PATH variable is correctly set via `envWithBin`, but the fundamental issue is that .cmd files need shell resolution. Fix: add `shell: true` to the spawnSync options. This allows the shell to resolve `tsc.cmd` on Windows while still working on Unix. The `envWithBin` PATH augmentation still works with `shell: true`. Also consider adding `stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']` explicitly (currently defaults to 'pipe' for all three, which is fine, but explicit is clearer). Note: the existing code at lines 1011-1015 already handles ENOENT — the fix should prevent it from happening in the first place.

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSON misleading error on 204 No Content responses with schema validation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want a clear error message when a 204 No Content response is received with a schema that expects content so that I can debug API integration issues easily.
acceptanceCriteria: When fetchJSON receives a 204 response and a schema is provided, the error message indicates the response had no content body rather than a generic 'schema validation failed' message
When fetchJSON receives a 204 response without a schema, undefined is returned (existing behavior preserved)
When fetchJSON receives a 200 response with a schema, normal validation applies (no regression)
userStories.notes: File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts, lines 104-123. The issue: when the response is 204 No Content, `data` is set to `undefined` (line 108). If a schema is provided, `schema.safeParse(undefined)` is called (line 118), which fails for any schema that doesn't accept undefined (like z.object({})). The error message says 'response from [URL] failed schema validation' which is misleading — the real issue is there was no response body. Fix: before the schema validation block (line 117), add a check: if `response.status === 204 && schema` then throw a ToolError.validation with a message like `'Expected JSON response from ${url} but received 204 No Content — the server returned no body to validate'`. This gives plugin developers a clear, actionable error. The check should go between the JSON parsing block and the schema validation block.
